Speaker 1: All right, so after you launch your campaign, you created your campaign, you made all
your different test ad sets, you created all your ads. Now we have to abide by something
called the four day law. So we set up our campaign, we set up our ad sets, we set up our
ads, and now we're just waiting to see if it works. Now in the very beginning, it can be
super tempting even for myself not to mess with it, right?
Speaker 1: You see something doesn't look like it's going to work and you want to turn it off
because you don't want to lose money. Or you see something that's working really good
and you want to up the budget 'cause you think it's gonna make you a lot of money. But
according to the four day law, you cannot touch any of your ad sets for four days. So
even if one's just tanking, it's losing you tons of money. You cannot turn it off for four
days. And even if one's making a ton of money, you can't raise the budget for four days.
Speaker 1: Now, the reason why is because of something called initial conditions. Now in the
beginning ads are just kind of figuring themselves out. The Facebook algorithm, which is
super complex, more complex than we can ever imagine, is trying to figure out which
direction to take your ad sets, who to show your ads to, who's most likely to buy and
you don't know if it's working or not 'til after four days. An ad that's absolutely just
tanking, losing you tons of money, might kind of get in a groove by the fourth day and
just start rocking and making you tons of money. And an ad that looks profitable from
day one might just tank on day four. So we have to wait for four days to figure out which
ones are truly working and which ones aren't. Because the data from a day or two days
is worthless. It doesn't mean anything. So do not touch your ads for the first four days.
Speaker 1: Whenever you create a new ad, this goes forever. Whenever you create a new ad set or
a new ad, you cannot touch them for four days until you see what happens. Which again
is why I said there can't be any last ditch efforts. Because if you have your last $20
you're just going to turn the ads off. If you have your last $20 and you see one's
working, you're going to hike the price up. So you need to be methodical about it. You
need to be super, just step by step methodical, knowing exactly what you're going to do.
